title: Hieracium melaneilema
taxonomy: Hieracium melaneilema is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification places it among the flowering plants, which are characterized by their showy blooms and composite flower heads. At the genus level, Hieracium melaneilema belongs to a group of plants known as hawkweeds, which are native to various parts of the world. The species is further distinguished by its unique characteristics, such as leaf shape and flower color. This classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hieracium melaneilema, also known as black hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion. The bioactive constituents of black hawkweed include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, black hawkweed is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs in remedies for various health conditions.
